description:
# AWSSupport-TroubleshootMSKClusterConnection

## Objective
Automated troubleshooting tool to diagnose and resolve Amazon MSK cluster connectivity issues including broker health problems, network configuration issues, and IAM authentication problems.

## Implementation Steps
1. Verify the execution role has all required permissions
2. Retrieve MSK cluster configuration and metadata
3. Analyze broker health metrics to identify performance issues
4. Deploy CloudFormation stack with VPC-attached Lambda for connectivity testing
5. Retrieve Lambda function details from created stack
6. Perform live DNS resolution and TCP connectivity test to MSK endpoint
7. Route workflow based on connectivity test results
8. Identify Lambda ENI for use as Reachability Analyzer source
9. Use VPC Reachability Analyzer to diagnose network connectivity issues
10. Fallback static analysis of network configuration when Lambda testing fails
11. Determine if IAM authentication analysis is needed based on port
12. Retrieve IAM policies attached to client role for analysis
13. Analyze IAM policies for required MSK permissions
14. Clean up CloudFormation stack and associated resources
15. Provide manual cleanup instructions if stack deletion fails
16. Compile comprehensive troubleshooting results with actionable recommendations.

## Important
This runbook uses [VPC Reachability Analyzer](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/reachability/what-is-reachability-analyzer.html) to diagnose network connectivity issues. Each analysis incurs a charge. For current pricing, see [VPC Reachability Analyzer pricing](https://aws.amazon.com/vpc/pricing/). To skip network path analysis and use static analysis only, ensure the Lambda connectivity test succeeds or the Reachability Analyzer step will be attempted automatically when connectivity fails.

ClusterARN.description: (Required) The ARN of the MSK cluster to troubleshoot.

BootstrapEndpoints.description: (Required) The MSK cluster bootstrap endpoint(s). Accepts a single endpoint in hostname:port format or multiple comma-separated endpoints (e.g., broker1:9092,broker2:9092,broker3:9092).

ClientVPC.description: (Required) The VPC ID where the client attempting to connect resides.

ClientSubnet.description: (Required) The subnet ID where the client attempting to connect resides.

ClientSecurityGroups.description: (Required) The security group ID(s) of the client attempting to connect. Accepts a single security group or multiple comma-separated security groups (e.g., sg-abc123,sg-def456).

LambdaAssumeRole.description: (Optional) IAM role ARN for Lambda function execution with VPC access permissions.

ClientIAMRole.description: (Optional) IAM role ARN used by the client for MSK authentication.

AutomationAssumeRole.description: (Optional) The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) role that allows Systems Manager Automation to perform the actions on your behalf. If no role is specified, Systems Manager Automation uses the permissions of the user that starts this runbook.

RetainResources.description: (Optional) Whether to retain Network Insights Analysis resources after completion.

mainSteps.name: VerifyPermissions
mainSteps.description: Verifies that the execution role has all required permissions before proceeding. This prevents partial resource creation (e.g., CloudFormation stacks that cannot be deleted) due to insufficient permissions.

mainSteps.name: DescribeCluster
mainSteps.description: Retrieves MSK cluster configuration and metadata to understand the cluster's current state and configuration.

mainSteps.name: CheckBrokerHealth
mainSteps.description: Analyzes broker health metrics from CloudWatch to identify performance issues such as high CPU, memory, disk usage, or exhausted credit balance.

mainSteps.name: UpdateBrokerHealthVariable
mainSteps.description: Updates the `BrokerHealthIssues` variable with output `HealthIssues` from `CheckBrokerHealth` step.

mainSteps.name: ParseBootstrapEndpoint
mainSteps.description: Parses the bootstrap endpoint to extract hostname and port for connectivity testing.

mainSteps.name: CreateConnectivityTestLambdaStack
mainSteps.description: Deploys a CloudFormation stack that creates a Lambda function in the client VPC to test connectivity to the MSK cluster. The Lambda function will be used to perform DNS resolution and TCP connectivity tests from within the client's network environment.

inputs.TemplateBody:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'Creates a Lambda function in the client VPC to test connectivity to the MSK cluster'

Parameters:
  ClientVPC:
    Type: String
    Description: The VPC ID where the client attempting to connect resides
    AllowedPattern: "^vpc-[a-z0-9]{8,17}$"
    
  ClientSubnet:
    Type: String
    Description: The subnet ID where the client attempting to connect resides
    AllowedPattern: "^subnet-[a-z0-9]{8,17}$"
    
  ClientSecurityGroups:
    Type: CommaDelimitedList
    Description: The security group ID(s) of the client attempting to connect
    
  LambdaAssumeRole:
    Type: String
    Description: IAM role ARN for Lambda function execution with VPC access permissions
    Default: ""

Conditions:
  CreateLambdaRole: !Equals [!Ref LambdaAssumeRole, ""]

Resources:
  LambdaExecutionRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Condition: CreateLambdaRole
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - !Sub 'arn:${AWS::Partition}:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole'
        - !Sub 'arn:${AWS::Partition}:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaVPCAccessExecutionRole'
      Tags:
        - Key: CreatedBy
          Value: 'AWSSupport-TroubleshootMSKClusterConnection'

  ENICleanupRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - !Sub 'arn:${AWS::Partition}:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole'
      Tags:
        - Key: CreatedBy
          Value: 'AWSSupport-TroubleshootMSKClusterConnection'
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: ENICleanupPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - ec2:DescribeNetworkInterfaces
                Resource: '*'
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - ec2:DeleteNetworkInterface
                Resource: !Sub 'arn:${AWS::Partition}:ec2:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:network-interface/*'
                Condition:
                  StringLike:
                    'ec2:Description': !Sub 'AWS Lambda VPC ENI-${ConnectivityTestLambda}*'
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - lambda:UpdateFunctionConfiguration
                Resource: !GetAtt ConnectivityTestLambda.Arn

  ENICleanupFunction:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      Handler: index.lambda_handler
      Role: !GetAtt ENICleanupRole.Arn
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import boto3
          import cfnresponse
          import time
          
          def lambda_handler(event, context):
              try:
                  if event['RequestType'] == 'Delete':
                      lambda_client = boto3.client('lambda')
                      ec2 = boto3.client('ec2')
                      lambda_function_name = event['ResourceProperties']['LambdaFunctionName']
                      
                      # Remove VPC configuration from Lambda function
                      try:
                          lambda_client.update_function_configuration(
                              FunctionName=lambda_function_name,
                              VpcConfig={
                                'SubnetIds': [],  
                                'SecurityGroupIds': []
                              }
                          )
                          print(f"Removed VPC configuration from {lambda_function_name}")
                          
                          # Wait for Lambda to detach from VPC
                          time.sleep(90)
                      except Exception as e:
                          print(f"Failed to remove VPC config: {str(e)}")
                      
                      # Find and delete ENIs
                      enis = []
                      slept_time = 0
                      while len(enis) == 0 and slept_time < 180:
                        time.sleep(30)
                        response = ec2.describe_network_interfaces(
                            Filters=[
                                {'Name': 'description', 'Values': [f'AWS Lambda VPC ENI-{lambda_function_name}*']},
                                {'Name': 'status', 'Values': ['available']}
                            ]
                        )
                        enis = response['NetworkInterfaces']
                        slept_time += 30

                      for eni in enis:
                          try:
                              ec2.delete_network_interface(NetworkInterfaceId=eni['NetworkInterfaceId'])
                              print(f"Deleted ENI: {eni['NetworkInterfaceId']}")
                          except Exception as e:
                              print(f"Failed to delete ENI {eni['NetworkInterfaceId']}: {str(e)}")
                  
                  cfnresponse.send(event, context, cfnresponse.SUCCESS, {})
              except Exception as e:
                  print(f"Error: {str(e)}")
                  cfnresponse.send(event, context, cfnresponse.FAILED, {})
      Runtime: python3.11
      Timeout: 300
      Tags:
        - Key: CreatedBy
          Value: 'AWSSupport-TroubleshootMSKClusterConnection'

  ENICleanup:
    Type: AWS::CloudFormation::CustomResource
    Properties:
      ServiceToken: !GetAtt ENICleanupFunction.Arn
      LambdaFunctionName: !Ref ConnectivityTestLambda

  ConnectivityTestLambda:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    DeletionPolicy: Delete
    Properties:
      Handler: index.lambda_handler
      Role: !If 
        - CreateLambdaRole
        - !GetAtt LambdaExecutionRole.Arn
        - !Ref LambdaAssumeRole
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import socket
          import json
          
          def lambda_handler(event, context):
              try:
                  endpoint = event['endpoint']
                  # Support multiple comma-separated endpoints; test all of them
                  endpoints = [ep.strip() for ep in endpoint.split(',')]
                  
                  all_reachable = True
                  first_resolved_ip = ''
                  all_issues = []
                  
                  for ep in endpoints:
                      hostname, port = ep.split(':')
                      port = int(port)
                      
                      # Perform DNS resolution
                      try:
                          resolved_ip = socket.gethostbyname(hostname)
                          if not first_resolved_ip:
                              first_resolved_ip = resolved_ip
                      except socket.gaierror as e:
                          all_reachable = False
                          all_issues.append(f'DNS resolution failed for {hostname}: {str(e)}')
                          continue
                      
                      # Test TCP connectivity
                      try:
                          sock = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
                          sock.settimeout(10)
                          result = sock.connect_ex((resolved_ip, port))
                          sock.close()
                          
                          if result != 0:
                              all_reachable = False
                              all_issues.append(f'TCP connection failed to {hostname} ({resolved_ip}:{port})')
                      except Exception as e:
                          all_reachable = False
                          all_issues.append(f'TCP connection test failed for {hostname}: {str(e)}')
                  
                  return {
                      'reachable': all_reachable,
                      'resolvedIP': first_resolved_ip,
                      'networkConnectivityIssues': all_issues
                  }
                      
              except Exception as e:
                  return {
                      'reachable': False,
                      'resolvedIP': '',
                      'networkConnectivityIssues': [f'Unexpected error: {str(e)}']
                  }
      Runtime: python3.11
      Timeout: 300
      MemorySize: 256
      VpcConfig:
        SecurityGroupIds: !Ref ClientSecurityGroups
        SubnetIds:
          - !Ref ClientSubnet
      Tags:
        - Key: CreatedBy
          Value: 'AWSSupport-TroubleshootMSKClusterConnection'

Outputs:
  LambdaFunctionArn:
    Description: The ARN of the Lambda function created for MSK connectivity testing
    Value: !GetAtt ConnectivityTestLambda.Arn
    
  LambdaFunctionName:
    Description: The name of the Lambda function created for MSK connectivity testing
    Value: !Ref ConnectivityTestLambda
    
  LambdaExecutionRoleArn:
    Description: The ARN of the IAM role created for the Lambda function (if created)
    Value: !If 
      - CreateLambdaRole
      - !GetAtt LambdaExecutionRole.Arn
      - !Ref LambdaAssumeRole

mainSteps.name: DescribeStackResources
mainSteps.description: Retrieves the outputs from the CloudFormation stack to get the Lambda function name for connectivity testing.

mainSteps.name: TestConnectivity
mainSteps.description: Invokes the Lambda function to test DNS resolution and TCP connectivity to the MSK bootstrap endpoint from within the client VPC.

mainSteps.name: UpdateConnectivityStatusVariable
mainSteps.description: Updates the `ConnectivityStatus` variable with output `Reachable` from `TestConnectivity` step.

mainSteps.name: UpdateConnectivityIssuesVariable
mainSteps.description: Updates the `ConnectivityIssues` variable with output `NetworkConnectivityIssues` from `TestConnectivity` step.

mainSteps.name: BranchOnConnectivity
mainSteps.description: Routes the workflow based on connectivity test results. If connectivity is successful, skips network analysis steps.

mainSteps.name: DescribeNetworkInterfaces
mainSteps.description: Identifies Lambda ENI for use as Reachability Analyzer source by querying network interfaces in the client subnet and security group.

mainSteps.name: AnalyzeNetworkPath
mainSteps.description: Uses VPC Reachability Analyzer to diagnose network connectivity issues between the client and MSK cluster.

mainSteps.name: UpdateNetworkAnalysisVariable
mainSteps.description: Updates the `NetworkAnalyses` variable with output `NetworkAnalyses` from `AnalyzeNetworkPath` step.

mainSteps.name: PerformStaticAnalysis
mainSteps.description: Performs fallback static analysis of network configuration when Lambda testing or Reachability Analyzer is unavailable. Analyzes route tables, NACLs, and security group rules.

mainSteps.name: UpdateStaticAnalysisVariable
mainSteps.description: Updates the `StaticAnalysisIssues` variable with output `Issues` from `PerformStaticAnalysis` step.

mainSteps.name: BranchOnBootstrapEndpoint
mainSteps.description: Determines if IAM authentication analysis is needed based on port and whether a client IAM role was provided.

mainSteps.name: AnalyzeIAMPolicy
mainSteps.description: Retrieves and analyzes IAM policies for required MSK permissions, particularly focusing on kafka-cluster:Connect permission which is essential for IAM authentication.

mainSteps.name: UpdateIAMVariable
mainSteps.description: Updates the `IAMAuthenticationIssues` variable with output `IAMAuthenticationIssues` from `AnalyzeIAMPolicy` step.

mainSteps.name: DeleteConnectivityTestLambdaStack
mainSteps.description: Cleans up the CloudFormation stack and associated resources created for connectivity testing.

mainSteps.name: ShareStackDeletionIntervention
mainSteps.description: Provides manual cleanup instructions if stack deletion fails, including steps to remove Lambda, IAM roles, and other resources.

mainSteps.name: FinalOutput
mainSteps.description: Compiles comprehensive troubleshooting results with actionable recommendations based on findings from previous steps.
